programmes.show_description: TV favourites James Bolam and Dennis Waterman team up to fight crime. To investigate a multi-million pound jewellery heist, Sandra and Gerry must pose as an unlikely couple.
Nonnatus House comes under new management in the shape of the formidable Sister Ursula (Harriet Walter), who begins making some drastic changes to the clinic.
Boiling Point: With Amsterdam almost empty of Jews, Miep and Jan do what they can to save the last remaining ones. Meanwhile, news of the Normandy landings arrives.
DI Mick Palmer, sole member of the Heritage Crime Unit, is dispatched to the imposing Astleigh House in Yorkshire, where art historian Francis Crozier has been murdered.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Parting: Time for a cuppa with Chigwell's most famous sisters. Sharon is faced with an impossible choice when her husband, Chris, tells her he is seriously ill.
Denial: Carver loses friends when the relief realises how much he has changed. Drinking to excess, he finally goes too far.
New. All in a Name: Joe bids farewell to the Mill and Letherbridge. A hidden crime is revealed when a teenage boy tries to hire Ronnie's services.
New. Lady Muck: Archie is enlisted to help save a man's life when a cleaner overhears her boss's murderous plans. At the Mill, Joe's presence is sorely missed.
Under Pressure: Tom's interviewed for the consultant post while Kath and Chrissie both apply for the same job. Lisa feels she's no longer one of the girls after reproaching Jess and Donna.
Entry Wounds Part 1: Nurse Mads struggles on the first day of her new job when shootings at Holby College throw a fully stretched ED into turmoil.
Denial: Carver loses friends when the relief realises how much he has changed. Drinking to excess, he finally goes too far.
New. Ronnie begins to fold under the pressure of the police investigation whilst Jack is torn between a rock and a hard place. Dot and Jim's anniversary party comes to an abrupt end.
New. Dot struggles to come to terms with Dotty's behaviour. Marsden's investigation hots up and the suspects begin to feel the pressure. Meanwhile, Ricky questions Bianca's loyalty.
Hail Caesar!: Nostalgic fun in the popular vet drama, based on James Herriot's books. The Herriots go in for a spot of highland dancing... and Siegfried catches some fleas.
Jack's life hangs in the balance as he is rushed to hospital on the Barracuda. Meanwhile, Ken's crafty ways backfire on him when he finds himself a victim of blackmail.
Too Cold for Hell: While tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters.
Nicked: The very first episode of the popular sitcom with the Chigwell sisters. Sharon and Tracey must adapt to a new life after their husbands are arrested for armed robbery.
Follow That Bottle: More laughs with those sprightly pensioners. Intrigued by a bottle with a message inside floating down the river, Truly decides that he has a duty to catch it.
How to Remove a Cousin: Roy Clarke's much-loved sitcom. Clegg's cousin Aubrey pays a not so welcome visit. The man is a health fanatic and takes a dim view of time-wasting walks!
Has Anyone Seen Barry's Midlife Crisis?: More wry humour, and some occasional slapstick, with those Yorkshire OAPs. Truly tries to spring the imprisoned Howard from his bedroom.
My Daughter, Right or Wrong: Adventures with our hunky High Court judge. An explosion in a lab kills an eminent scientist, and an animal rights activist is put on trial.
TV favourites James Bolam and Dennis Waterman team up to fight crime. To investigate a multi-million pound jewellery heist, Sandra and Gerry must pose as an unlikely couple.
Too Cold for Hell: While tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters.
A Game of Soldiers: A feature-length mystery for the detectives. The apparently motiveless murder of an American tourist brings Dalziel back into contact with an old flame.
Parting: Time for a cuppa with Chigwell's most famous sisters. Sharon is faced with an impossible choice when her husband, Chris, tells her he is seriously ill.
The Hope Too Bright to Last: Corcoran finds himself at an unusual dead end. Doctor Freeman is faced with the outbreak of a new and troubling illness sweeping Five Points.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Jobs for the Girls: Chigwell comedy with the Essex girls. Sharon and Tracey want spending money for Christmas, so they take up some extra work to pay for last minute presents.
Walking the Line: The normally moderate Duncan Lennox is outraged to be accused of racism by a suspected robber.
New. Stabbed in the Back: Daniel steps in to save the surgery when an angry patient threatens to drive a truck into the building after discovering his wife's affair.
New. Eye of the Beholder: When Michelle visits a reclusive spinster, she discovers the woman has been hiding a son with a facial deformity.
Pastures New: Kath decides to leave Holby - but before going, she admits to Lisa that she helped Terry die. Mubbs lashes out at one of Rosie's daughters, and George gets another new heart.
Entry Wounds Part 2: The aftermath of the shootings and the dramatic incidents at Holby College leave everyone in the emergency department struggling to cope.
Walking the Line: The normally moderate Duncan Lennox is outraged to be accused of racism by a suspected robber.
New. Bradley is terrified the police will discover his secret, while Shirley wonders if she can trust Phil. Bianca is excited with wedding preparations as surprise guests arrive.
New. The police receive an anonymous tip-off, but will they arrest the right person? Ian's suspicious behaviour raises questions for Lucy, and Max worries about Jack's plan.
Only One Woof: Peter Davison, the fifth Doctor Who, stars in the veterinary drama. James and Siegfried deal with a sheepdog trainer whose dog is suffering from epilepsy.
Jan is surprised to learn she has a business rival, and The Orkadian has its official launch at the Mermaid Yard - without Jack.
How The Rogue Roar'd: When Frank and Lu are hired to help close down an old bingo hall, Frank is surprised to find an old nemesis at work.
Just Visiting: Sharon is rejoicing in her new-found freedom, while Tracey is lost without Darryl and is wondering how she is going to break the news of his crime to their son Garth.
The Genuine Outdoors Robin Hood Barbi: Roy Clarke's record breaking sitcom. Billy takes Clegg, Truly, Alvin and Entwistle out for a real barbecue in the real outdoors.
Barry in Danger from Reading and Aunt Jessie: The sitcom that's long in the tooth but has a spring in its step! Nora Batty enquires about the mystery woman seen with Alvin.
Who's That Merry Man with Billy, Then?: The world's longest-running sitcom! Recruitment for Billy's Merry Men is at a low ebb, until he receives a letter from an enthusiast.
Girl, Whitstable Beach: After seven years, a missing young mum is spotted on Whitstable Beach. Pearl wants to know where she's gone and why she's back.
Death, the Devil and the Fool: When a young girl goes missing at the Whitstable Psychic Fair, a sceptical Pearl has to join forces with a clairvoyant to help find her.
Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong star in the light-hearted crime series. When Gerry loses badly at poker, he agrees to pay his debt by investigating the death of a bookie.
